reviews.title: No service at all
reviews.text:
What you paid is JUST for a space, don't expect anything else at all. Requested slippers for 5 times to have them delivered on the next day. Dusty everywhere in the room like it's been vacant for ages (well, but request for early check in was turned down). Water was given only on your arrival but not the other days of your stay, you almost need to beg them in order to get an extra bottle. Not a single smile was found on the staff during the stay. No housekeeping service during night times. 
Wifi so weak that it hardly send through a message. So other than they did give you a room, there's nothing more.  Simply don't choose this hotel unless you are too happy recently and is looking for something to ruin your mood.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: Bed was having blood stain and different stain all over the bed

reviews.title: Disappointed
reviews.text: Room looks old, desk and carpet not very clean, no slippers or toiletry or bottled watter provided, shower very badly designed as all the water will spill everywhere despite one being careful, really uncomfortable...not what one would normally expect from a hotel claiming to be four stars. Half of the building is sharing with another hotel, which is weird but fine. Problem is literally saw someone looking for things in the trashcan outside, then  came in the hotel, talked to the staff at the front desk, and took the lift at the lobby, felt like there was no security whatsoever. Few good things are at least shower is hot enough, bed is actually alright and restaurant nearby is good, transportation is supposed to be alright but considering all the other aspect not worth staying here, max. two stars hotel.

reviews.title: Overbooking
reviews.text: Very bad at check in as hotel staff inform me there is no room even though the payment has been made and confirmed thru Agoda. Staff is trying to offer me to stay in another hotel very far from this location and try to arrange the taxi for me to go. I am not happy with this bad management in the late evening which I have to fight for it until they release the room for me at the end. How come customer needs to fight for the room which has been already confirmed booking and paid???

reviews.title: Locked door 3-4 times a day
reviews.text:
When I checkin on 15/3, the staff did not tell me I have tonpay City tax. At night, I received an email and asked to settle in reception. I read and plan to go when available.  Since I have to go Rotterdam  on 16/3, when I come back to Hotel and want to take a rest first, I found I can't use my key card to open door. Then, I go to reception and the key card was unlocked. At this moment, not yet settle payment. Then, on another date our door was being locked again, and your staff explained that the card always inactive when we put the card with wallet and phone. We really don't believe, since after we do the payment, the card function properly. I think it is your staff problem not ask me to complete payment when check-in, but your hotel always deactivate the card and locked the door is impolite and cause us very inconvenient. 

In addition, water always flow out from shower area. Please improve

Hope your hotel can make compensation to us. Your arrangement made us feel embarrassing.

reviews.title: Don’t accept room 319 or a room on the 3rd floor.
reviews.positives: We thought the property was amazing, until we used the shower. The shower in our room made a horrific noise that could be heard across the entire floor. The entire time it was in use it was like an air horn blowing nonstop. Maintenance came to look but they, along with the front desk, offered no solution. Everyone also acted like this wasn’t a known issue…but it had to be.  It disrupted the entire floor and with three of us showering each day, it had to be an awful wake up call an hour at a time. I wish I could attach the video to this review!

reviews.title: The performance of hotel people is way below average.   More training are needed.
reviews.text:
Two disappointments for staying this hotel for 2 nights.
1) Reported the minibar storage stayed at room temperature immediately right after checking into my room in the hotel.  However, the front desk lady insisted that was normal and failed to have technicians to come over to check and fix the problem.
2) The cleaning lady kept bugging us for cleaning our room the next morning.   Asked her to come back at 12:30pm, she said she has done with other rooms and is ready to go home.    The room was not cleaned for that day when we come back late that night.     What a 4-star hotel!!

We had left the original towels on the floor of the bathroom, so they were soaked. The hotel is not cheap and this is pretty basic, so actually quite disappointed.

reviews.title: Not Mercure Standard
reviews.text: Having been stayed in many Mercure hotels, this hotel is among the worst. The lobby was ok but there was strange smell in the corridor at the guest room level (weed?).  The corridor as well as the room were far too dimly lit, creating a gloomy and unpleasant atmosphere.  The room was ok but definitely not the size that was posted on Agoda.  On the other hand, the location is good, just a couple of minutes walk to the railway station or the bus/tram station.  I would try other hotels nearby next time.

reviews.title: Average hotel, good for  quick layovers
reviews.positives: It's an average hotel. Maybe be good for a layover but far from ideal to explore Amsterdam. You got to take a train to get to the main city. Average room, a little bit dark and cold. The best thing is breakfast, the breakfast is AMAZING.

reviews.title: Location is not quite convenient
reviews.text:
While it is located at sloterdijk station which is only one station from Centraal station by train, it is not as convenient as expected. Especially if you are using the GVB day pass, you can only choose one bus route and take around half an hour to back and forth from the hotel and the centraal station. There are not many shops and restaurant near the hotel.

Nevertheless, the staff in the hotel is friendly and helpful. And the facilities in the room is quite good.

But taking into accout the transportation, I think it is not a good choice for travellers going arpund the spots in Amsterdam.

The only downside was the service (or lack thereof) that we received at the Sky Bar restaurant. We went there on 4/27/24 at 10:45pm, looking forward to having a drink before going back to our room. We walked in, saw other people dining, and were told they were no longer serving drinks at the bar as they closed at 11:00pm. This was very surprising to us as 15 minutes would’ve been more than enough time to finish one drink. The two female workers who told us this were unfriendly, and were chatting together at the bar when we walked in. So, it seemed it was more an issue of us interrupting their social time together rather than them not being able to pour two beers in a matter of 15 minutes.
On that note, we highly recommend Marmaris Grill & Pizza across the street. They are open late, have great food, and their staff are awesome!!!

reviews.title: It was a hotel where the good and the bad were beautifully mixed.
reviews.text:
As many reviews have mentioned, the breakfast at the restaurant on the top floor (8th floor) was truly satisfying. The variety and deliciousness were impressive, and though there are busy times, I felt the table arrangement was well done. Considering it as a hotel breakfast buffet, I think the price is reasonable.

② It's rare for hotels abroad, but the usability of the shower was excellent! I had no complaints about the water pressure or temperature control; it was a great shower.

③ Having a refrigerator is also a plus.

However, one of the two windows did not have a sheer blind, and when I raised the blackout blind, the outside was fully visible (presumably the same from the outside). I tried to use the laundry corner, but to operate it, I had to download a dedicated app and pre-charge €20. The usage fee for each wash and dry cycle was €5.50. It might be good if you are staying for a long time. By the way, there was no explanation or list regarding the facilities in the room.

Moreover, the refrigerator had a leftover fruit drink bottle from the previous guest stuck sideways, which meant I had to deal with that before getting the welcome drink (water), making for a disappointing start.

I intentionally chose to stay next to the central station to avoid the noise, but every time I bought a ticket, there was a €1.50 issuance fee. I tried to buy an e-ticket but couldn't. Since I had round-trip tickets, the issuance fee was €3 for two tickets, but that still felt like a waste of about 500 yen daily.

What hurt the most was that the room door's lock never locked on the first attempt (even if the door closed, it could still be opened from the hallway or the room without being locked). On the day of check-out, I spoke with the manager, but conveying that in English without a translation app with my child was extremely challenging. If I had mentioned it on the first day, room arrangements (changing to a different room) could have been made, and repairs could have been done along the way. Bringing this up on check-out day when there's nothing that could be done was frustrating.

You're absolutely right. Really. However, if I had the language skills to respond back, in an email, I could say that on the first day, after more than 15 hours traveling straight from Japan to check-in at 10:30 PM, both my child and I were exhausted and just wanted to lie down and eat something. I pressed every button of the provided phone to connect to the front desk, but it didn't connect (the display was in Dutch).

Since my stay was only for three days, I left early the next morning, and of course, by the last day, both the room and the bathroom had been completely customized. When it comes to communicating about the door key in this short stay, the first day was the only time I had, and considering the fatigue from that day, I couldn't manage to communicate about it. In the end, I was only able to film a video showing that the key didn't lock, which I regretted as it reflected my lack of ability.

I’d say there’s no reason to choose this hotel again based on my experience.

Additionally, the issue with the window lacking a lace blind and the unclear in-room facility information are points we will review internally to improve the guest experience.

Regarding the laundry system, we understand that downloading an app and paying in advance may be inconvenient for short stays, and we will be reevaluati

It is also close to Amsterdam, and you can reach Zaanse Schans in just two stations from Sloterdijk. The amenities include coffee, tea, shampoo, and body soap, and towels are provided, but you will need to bring other essentials. However, there is no coin laundry nearby, so I hand-washed small items, but they hardly dried because it didn't feel like the ventilation fan was working in the room. I found it unusual in dry Europe. If you don't expect the type of service like in Japan that caters to every need, it is a very good hotel.

reviews.title: Good value for money
reviews.text:
It is conveniently Located right next to Sloterdijk station.  The only catchbis that traveling from the hotel to Amsterdam centraal is faster using train which is not included in GVB ticket pass.
Hotel is clean, and quiet. Staff is friendly.

Some minor improvement could be good as there is no toothpaste and toothbrush provided. Room service could be improved to check for empty shampoo or body wash as ours were empty on arrival and the next day. The bottle was only filled when it was asked.  We always got home very late, so we just hesitated to ask for refill in the middle of the night. But after day 2 , we had to make the request at 1am.

Overall, it is a great stay. I will be likely to stay here again.

A few restaurants were in the area.  Room was quiet.
reviews.negatives: Breakfast was expensive, which we didn't get.  We were leaving for the airport early the next day anyway.

I was assigned a room on the first floor, and when I opened the curtains, I could see people walking on the street, so I kept the curtains closed the whole time. Nevertheless, the room was large and comfortable, and the shower booth was very spacious, which was nice.
reviews.response:
Dear Son,

Thank you very much for sharing your detailed feedback regarding your recent stay at Mercure Hotel Amsterdam Sloterdijk Station.

We are pleased to read that you found your room spacious and comfortable, and that you particularly enjoyed the large shower. It is always rewarding to know that our facilities contributed positively to your stay.

Our location next to Sloterdijk Station indeed offers a convenient alternative for reaching the hotel, especially when transportation options in the city can be limited at times. We appreciate you highlighting this for future travelers.

At the same time, we understand your comments regarding the first-floor room and privacy when opening the curtains. Please do not hesitate to inform our reception team during a future stay, as we would be happy to check availability for a room on a higher floor to better suit your preferences.

We truly appreciate your balanced feedback and hope to have the pleasure of welcoming you again soon.

Kind re

reviews.positives: The place was absolutely beautiful and super clean. The staff was the best. Gideon & Galil were the best! They were helpful guiding us to where ever we had to go. They took very good care of us and the staff at the restaurant were very nice and helpful.

reviews.title: Nice stay
reviews.text:
Stayed for 4 nights. It’s located in a convenient location near the train station. The bus station is also nearby. There’s a SPAR convenience store close by to stock up on water and amenities as the hotel does not provide any. However, there is a kettle to make tea or coffee which is provided in the room. 

The room was a bit dim and the bed was soft and quite high. There’s a fancy chandelier that hangs just right above the bed so we got to be careful each time we go to bed.

The room was quite small so we couldn’t fully open our luggages without obstructing the way. 

The bathroom is very nice with a mirror with ring lights.  There was a bit of flooding after shower so be careful it can be slippery. Also remember to not hang your towels if you want new ones. 

Overall, we had a great stay. The staff were very nice and even greeted us happy birthday when we checked in. Thanks, Mercure Hotel!

reviews.text: The hotel is in amazing location. There is a Turkish Italian restaurant ans market literally next to the train station which is 3 mins walk from the hotel. Can’t comment on breakfast hence we did not have any. Cleaning was ok. I liked getting new and dry towels hence they were not really drying easily in the room. Free water was a nice gedture on the day of arrival. Yes, it was a fun week for us!

With kind regards, 

Nick den Hartog

reviews.title: Great location !!
reviews.text:
Situated right next to the station and transport hub (bus, metro, tram) making it easy to connect to everywhere. The hotel is a few minutes walk from the Sprinter trains to and from the airport.
There are also ample food options and a supermarket within walking distance. Room was spacious though the bathroom probably needs some changes; the room I was in did not have a shower door and the angle which the shower head was installed will cause the whole bathroom to get wet after showering.
Tip - I am seeing some public transport disruptions resulting in bus, tram and train delays so ensure sufficient time when planning travel.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives:
Right next to the Amsterdam Sloterdijk station (5-10 min walk), Hotel Mercure is a great spot for an overnight stay without the prices you'd find in the heart of Amsterdam.
With the savoury Wissenkerk restaurant on floor -1, and the restaurant bar with a breakfast buffet for €23 that would put any American chain to shame, there is plenty of options for any hungry traveler (like me).
The rooms feature COLD AC, comfortable beds, and spacious bathroom with shower (unlike what you typically find in Europe).
And if you're a traveler looking for some rest and relaxation in the room, the large LCD TV with 40+ channels covering Dutch, English, Russian, Spanish and more.
Highly recommended from a friend, did not disappoint, and will make it top of my list next time in Amsterdam

reviews.title: wonderful support

They went even extra miles to send me a taxi when I found myself in an unfamiliar place due to the unexpected close down of  one train station.  A carefully selected breakfast was awaiting in my room at my arrival as I did not get the opportunity to eat for over 22 hours. The team, but actually I have to say a really kind lady at the reception (unfortunately I don't remember her name) has left a really kind note in the room. I surely appreciate her gesture. She was modest enough to sign the  Mercure team despite she did it all. I recommande this hotel for the kindness of the staff, the location as I found it ideal, just 2 stations away from both Airport and Amsterdam Central.
